2606.23758.title: Exploring Dualistic Meta-Learning to Enhance Domain Generalization in Open Set Scenarios
2606.23758.paper_summary: Domain generalization learns from multiple source domains to generalize to unseen target domains. However, it often neglects the realistic case of label mismatch between source and target. Open set domain generalization is then proposed to recognize unseen classes in unseen domains. A simple approach trains one-vs-all classifiers to separate each class and detect outliers as unknown. Yet, the imbalance between few positive samples and many negative samples skews the decision boundary towards the positive ones, leading the model to over-reject out-of-distribution data, even from known classes in unseen domains. In this paper, we propose a novel meta-learning stategy called dualistic MEta-learning with joint DomaIn-Class matching (MEDIC), which considers implicit gradient matching towards inter-domain and inter-class task splits simultaneously to find optimal boundaries balanced for both domains and classes. Experimental results show that MEDIC not only outperforms prior methods in open set scenarios, but also maintains competitive close set generalization ability.
2606.23758.paper_summary_zh: 領域泛化從多個來源領域學習，以便對未見的目標領域進行泛化。<lb/>然而，它經常忽略來源和目標之間標籤不匹配的現實情況。<lb/>因此，提出了開放集領域泛化，以識別未見領域中的未見類別。<lb/>一種簡單的方法訓練一對多的分類器來區分每個類別，並將異常檢測為未知。<lb/>然而，少量正樣本和大量負樣本之間的不平衡使決策邊界偏向正樣本，導致模型過度拒絕分佈外的數據，即使是來自未見領域的已知類別。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一種新穎的元學習策略，稱為雙重元學習與聯合領域類別匹配（MEDIC），它同時考慮對於跨領域和跨類別任務拆分的隱式梯度匹配，以找到對兩個領域和類別都平衡的最佳邊界。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，MEDIC 不僅在開放集場景中超越了先前的方法，還保持了具有競爭力的封閉集泛化能力。
2606.23758.author: Xiran Wang et.al.
2606.23758.authors: Xiran Wang, Jian Zhang, Lei Qi, Yang Gao, Yinghuan Shi
